title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Automated Funds Transfer (AFT)
An Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) is a Debit or Credit Transaction. Debit Transactions are known as Pre-authorized Debits (PADs). Credit Transactions are known as Direct Deposits.
BC Employment and Assistance Cheque
A BC Employment and Assistance Cheque is a monthly cheque issued by the Ministry of Social Development and Poverty Reduction to provide income assistance to British Columbians.
Branch Number
The Branch Number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number.
Business PAD
A Business PAD is a PAD drawn on the account of a Payor for the payment of goods or services related to a business or commercial activity of the Payor including, but not limited to, payments between franchisees and franchisers, distributors and suppliers, and dealers and manufacturers.
Cash Management PAD
A Cash Management PAD is a Pre-authorized Debit drawn on the account of a Payor for the purpose of transferring, consolidating, or repositioning funds between the Payorâs account at one financial institution to his or her account at another financial institution, for the same business or closely affiliated businesses (e.g., transfers between a parent company and its subsidiary).
Chargeback
A Chargeback is an item that your financial institution (as the Negotiating Financial Institution) deposits that is subsequently dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution.
Charter Number
The first 3 digits of a Branch Number.
Clearing Replacement Document
Clearing Replacement Documents (CRDs) are official documents that meet the specifications of CPA Standard 014, Clearing Replacement Document Design Standard. CRDs can be used in place of an original paper payment item for presentment and return purposes in accordance with CPA Rule A10, Images, Image Printouts and Return Replacement Documents.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
Drawee
A Drawee is the financial institution to which a debit payment item is addressed. The Drawee is directed to pay the specified amount of money.
Drawer
A Drawer is the person who signs the cheque ordering the Drawee financial institution to pay the specified amount of money stated on it.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Incoming Clearing Item
An Incoming Clearing Item is a cheque, draft, or money order that is drawn on your financial institution or on a customer of your financial institution, deposited by the Payee, and sent through Clearing for debit to the Drawerâs account.
Institution Number
The Institution Number is a 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies a financial institution (e.g., 809 for BC credit unions and 828 for Ontario credit unions). The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number. The Institution Number is sometimes known as the route number.
Item in Dispute
Item in Dispute means a returned item that a Negotiating Financial Institution believes wasnât returned in accordance with the CPA rules.
Negotiating Financial Institution
Negotiating Financial Institution means the financial institution that presents an item for payment to the Drawee.
Payee
The Payee is the person or entity to whom funds are to be paid.
Payor
The Payor is the person or entity whose account is debited.
Personal PAD
A Personal PAD is a PAD drawn on the account of a Payor for payments such as, but not limited to, charitable donations, non-Member investment contributions, mortgage installments, utility bills, insurance premiums, membership fees, property taxes, credit card billings, lines of credit, loans, and payment for other consumer goods or services but not a Business PAD, a Cash Management PAD, or a Funds Transfer PAD.
Pre-authorized Debit (PAD)
A Pre-authorized Debit (PAD) is an AFT transaction issued by a Payee or Member Payee that is drawn on an account of a Payor (e.g., a mortgage payment, strata payment).
Qualified Item
Qualified Items are cheques or drafts that meet CPA standards and are MICR-encoded with the Transit Number and dollar value. Refer to CPA, Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items.
Transit Number
The Transit Number consists of the Branch Number (5 digits) plus the Institution Number (3 digits). Each financial institution has a unique Transit Number (e.g., 80912010).
Unqualified Item
Unqualified Items are cheques or drafts that donât meet CPA standards for a Qualified Item or canât be imaged by Central 1âs imaging equipment. Central 1 determines whether an item is Qualified or Unqualified.
END
